SOXX.data_anomaly: price_source_conflict: fast_info prev_close $570.61 vs history $539.77 diverge by 5.7%. Signal suppressed.

macro_summary: Stocks steadied Monday with SPY +0.06% and QQQ +0.09% as the Hormuz crisis de-escalated — WTI slipped to $89.68 after Iran signalled an end to attacks on Israel. Semis surged (SOXX +5.87% on a broad rebound) but the move is flagged by a data anomaly. Meanwhile Intel jumped 12% on reports of foundry wins with Google and Nvidia, and the Pentagon added Alibaba and BYD to its military blacklist. Markets now brace for May CPI on Wednesday with the Fed funds rate at 3.62% and the 10Y at 4.55%.
commodities_note: WTI eased to $89.68 (-0.5%) as Iran hinted at de-escalation, though the Hormuz crisis enters its 98th day. Gold slipped 0.3% to $4,347. DXY was flat at 99.94. SOXX data shows a price-source conflict — the fast-info close of $570.61 versus a history close of $539.77 diverges by 5.7%, so the +5.87% daily change is unreliable. Use the index level and broader semis cluster price action for trend signals.

singapore.summary: The Singapore banks remain in a structural uptrend but are now extended — DBS is CAUTION on RSI 66, OCBC is WATCH as the relative NIM outperformer, and UOB consolidates near resistance. The dual NIM headwind (US10Y at 4.55%, MAS S$NEER on hold through October) hasn't eased, so the cluster is trading on rate-cut hope rather than margin relief. OCBC's 5.2% dividend yield is the only cushion here.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically extended — no NIM relief before Q4 2026
singapore.key_development: No fresh sector news; US10Y at 4.55% keeps NIM compression intact for day 74 of the standoff.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster staged a violent 5.9% bounce (SOXX to 571.45) after the AVGO-driven rout, but the rally was led by short-squeeze dynamics — 12 of 27 names are still hard-blocked on extension. Intel's Google 3M-chip foundry win and SK Hynix's NVDA partnership were the two most potent catalysts, but extreme extension (AMD +35%, MU +51%, 000660 +48%) makes nearly the entire cluster untradeable. Only TSEM and BE emerged as watchable entry candidates, both with the valuation/liquidity caveats required.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally intact, but the extension regime is the dominant tactical force — earnings must catch up to prices
semiconductors.key_development: Intel surged 12% on Google TPU order + Nvidia 18A trial (per TheStreet, Jun 9), while SK Hynix advanced 16% on a multiyear NVDA partnership (per Simply Wall St., Jun 9).

big_tech.summary: BigTech held flat as the AI capex debate deepened — Amazon's multibillion Corning fiber deal and Microsoft's NHS Copilot win show real infrastructure spend, but AVGO's forecast miss still casts a shadow. GOOG screens best with a 5.4:1 R:R to $404, but the wide-stop math is underwhelming. MSFT's pullback to the 50-day is promising but the stop is too tight. The cluster is in a waiting pattern ahead of CPI tomorrow.
big_tech.thesis_status: Cautiously constructive — capex is real, but the near-term entry math is not cooperating
big_tech.key_development: Amazon announced a multibillion-dollar Corning fiber supply deal (per Simply Wall St., Jun 9), and OpenAI filed for an IPO, potentially reducing Azure concentration risk for MSFT.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC design wins (Amazon Trainium, Google Axion) are the direct competitive read on AVGO — as hyperscalers diversify ASIC suppliers, MRVL gains share.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if MRVL pulls back to its 50-day average and holds above its structural support, likely around $80-85 based on recent price action.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: NVDA's $2B photonics investment counterpart — Coherent shares the AI optics demand tailwind with LITE and benefits from the same NVDA ecosystem expansion.
interconnected.entry_note: Watch for a cooldown toward the 50-day; add if it pulls back 8-10% from current levels and NVDA's photonics narrative strengthens.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: Primary supplier of AI datacenter switching to MSFT and Meta — hyperscaler capex directly flows into ANET's revenue, making it a clean read on AI infrastructure spend.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if hyperscaler capex commentary (especially MSFT's next earnings) confirms switching demand acceleration.

interconnected.name: Corning
interconnected.reason: Amazon's multibillion-dollar optical fiber deal directly ties GLW's revenue to AMZN's $200B AI data center buildout — a clear first-order connection.
interconnected.entry_note: Monitor for a 5-8% pullback from the post-announcement pop; entry becomes attractive if the stock consolidates near its 50-day.

active_risks: Hormuz blockade day 98 — Iran signaled end of attacks on Israel, but the Strait remains effectively closed to commercial oil transit; WTI at $89.68.
US-China tech blacklist — the Pentagon added Alibaba, BYD, and Baidu to its military company list, widening restrictions beyond semis; China's May exports surged 19.4% on front-loaded chip orders.
Houthis threaten Israeli Red Sea shipping — the Yemen-based group announced a total ban on Israeli-affiliated vessels, adding a new layer of maritime risk.
Fed rate expectations — 10Y at 4.55%, Fed funds at 3.62%; markets price in a potential hike with CPI due Wednesday, keeping upward pressure on real yields.
geopolitical.market_impact: De-escalation in the Middle East and China's export surge provided a floor for equities, but the tech blacklist expansion and Houthi threat cap the relief. Semis rallied on the back of that relief, but extreme extensions mean the move is fragile. Singapore banks remain under NIM pressure with US10Y above 4.20%.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Iran signaled an end to attacks on Israel (tentative de-escalation); Pentagon added Alibaba, BYD, and Baidu to its military blacklist; OpenAI filed confidentially for an IPO; Houthis threatened Israeli Red Sea shipping.

base.description: Hormuz stalemate holds at current levels (WTI ~$90), and the Fed stays on hold through summer. Semis consolidate their gains and rotation into value/defense persists, keeping DBS, OCBC, and UOB supported at rich valuations. BigTech remains range-bound ahead of CPI.

optimistic.description: Hormuz de-escalation deepens — WTI falls below $85, removing the inflation impulse. CPI comes in soft, halting rate-hike bets, and US10Y drops toward 4.0%. Semis rally further, and Singapore banks lift as NIM relief becomes plausible.

pessimistic.description: Hormuz reignites, WTI spikes above $100, and the Houthi Red Sea ban escalates. CPI prints hot, forcing a July rate hike. Semis sell off sharply, and Singapore banks break their 50-day averages as the yield curve steepens.

wildcard.description: The Pentagon blacklist triggers a full-scale US-China investment freeze, or OpenAI's IPO collapses the AI funding bubble. A systemic risk-off event drives gold above $4,500 and crushes tech multiples, breaking all watchlist technical supports.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold cash; the extension regime across semis and Singapore banks prevents new entries today. Reassess after Wednesday's CPI — a soft print could open selective entry points in BE and GOOG.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Cautious — extreme extension regime and upcoming CPI prevent new entries
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 12 of 27 names are hard-blocked on technical extension — the broad semis surge after the AVGO rout added to existing overbought conditions. The CPI print tomorrow is the next binary: a hot number could break the fragile bounce and send 10+ names to deeper CAUTION or AVOID.

scheduled_tech_events.notes: Vera Rubin samples confirmed shipping; optics integration revealed in Rubin rack design. Blackwell Ultra → Rubin transition timeline given.
Taiwan AI infrastructure partnerships; CoWoS capacity commitments from TSMC; HBM4 roadmap. Direct thesis-confirming event for AI Power Infrastructure cluster.
AI kit, on-device model integration, Vision Pro 2 rumours. Competitive signal for MSFT Copilot and GOOG Gemini on-device.

contradictions.sentence: Markets now brace for May CPI on Wednesday with the Fed funds rate at 3.62% and the 10Y at 4.55%.

macro_context_line: Rate backdrop (FRED, not live): payrolls +172k (eased from +179k); unemployment 4.3% (flat). Reconcile the rate lean in the macro_summary against this when a CPI/FOMC/jobs event is near.

taxonomy_discrimination.note: Output-only meta-analysis of signal-class discrimination. NOT injected into the analysis prompt and NOT persisted into memory.lessons_learned. full_corpus pools pre-cutover (different-instrument) rows — informational only.

calibration_insights.confidence_banner: Actionable buckets BUY (n=4) / ACCUMULATE (n=11) spanning chop, trend_up. NOT yet decision-grade — alpha figures are benchmark-relative; single-regime samples carry no out-of-regime evidence.
calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
